question_text_combined: "Rapid Financing Instrument" and "Rapid Credit Facility" are related to the provisions of lending by which one of the following? [cite: 47]
options_combined: United Nations Environment Programme Finance Initiative
answer_explanation: The Rapid Financing Instrument (RFI) and Rapid Credit Facility (RCF) are lending facilities created by the International Monetary Fund (IMF). They provide rapid financial assistance to member countries facing urgent balance of payments needs, with the RCF specifically offering concessional support to low-income countries.
answer_sources: IMF Official Website: Rapid Financing Instrument (RFI) Factsheet
IMF Official Website: Rapid Credit Facility (RCF) Factsheet
subject: Economy

question_text_combined: With reference to the Indian economy, consider the following statements: 1. An increase in Nominal Effective Exchange Rate (NEER) indicates the appreciation of rupee. 2. An increase in the Real Effective Exchange Rate (REER) indicates an improvement in trade competitiveness. 3. An increasing trend in domestic inflation relative to inflation in other countries is likely to cause an increasing divergence between NEER and REER. [cite_start]Which of the above statements are correct? [cite: 47]
answer_explanation: Statement 1 is correct; an increase in NEER denotes an appreciation of the domestic currency against a weighted basket of foreign currencies. Statement 2 is incorrect; an increase in REER means exports are becoming relatively more expensive, indicating a loss in trade competitiveness. Statement 3 is correct; because REER adjusts NEER for relative inflation differentials, sustained higher domestic inflation causes the two metrics to diverge.
answer_sources: RBI Bulletin: Real Effective Exchange Rate (REER) Methodology
NCERT Class 12 Macroeconomics: Open Economy Macroeconomics
subject: Economy

question_text_combined: With reference to the Indian economy, consider the following statements: 1. If the inflation is too high, Reserve Bank of India (RBI) is likely to buy government securities. 2. If the rupee is rapidly depreciating, RBI is likely to sell dollars in the market. 3. If interest rates in the USA or European Union were to fall, that is likely to induce RBI to buy dollars. [cite_start]Which of the statements given above are correct? [cite: 47]
answer_explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect; to combat high inflation, the RBI sells government securities (Open Market Operations) to absorb excess liquidity from the system. Statement 2 is correct; the RBI sells dollars from its forex reserves to increase dollar supply and support a rapidly depreciating rupee. Statement 3 is correct; falling interest rates abroad drive foreign capital inflows into India seeking higher yields, causing the rupee to appreciate, which induces the RBI to buy dollars to stabilize the currency.
answer_sources: RBI Core Monetary Policy Operations Guidelines
subject: Economy

question_text_combined: With reference to the "G20 Common Framework", consider the following statements: 1. It is an initiative endorsed by the G20 together with the Paris Club. 2. It is an initiative to support Low Income Countries with unsustainable debt. [cite_start]Which of the statements given above is/are correct? [cite: 47]
answer_explanation: Statement 1 is correct; the Common Framework for Debt Treatments was endorsed jointly by the G20 and the Paris Club. Statement 2 is correct; the initiative specifically aims to help Low-Income Countries (LICs) restructure their debt to address insolvency and protracted liquidity problems.
answer_sources: World Bank/IMF joint publication on G20 Common Framework for Debt Treatments
subject: International Relations

question_text_combined: With reference to the Indian economy, what are the advantages of "Inflation-Indexed Bonds (IIBs)"? 1. Government can reduce the coupon rates on its borrowing by way of IIBs. 2. IIBs provide protection to the investors from uncertainty regarding inflation. 3. The interest received as well as capital gains on IIBs are not taxable. [cite_start]Which of the statements given above are correct? [cite: 98]
answer_explanation: Statement 1 is correct; since IIBs eliminate the inflation risk premium for investors, the government can issue them at lower initial coupon rates. Statement 2 is correct; IIBs index the principal to inflation, protecting real investor returns. Statement 3 is incorrect; in India, the interest and capital gains accrued on IIBs are fully taxable under prevailing income tax laws.
answer_sources: RBI Notification on Inflation Indexed Bonds (IIBs)
subject: Economy

question_text_combined: With reference to foreign-owned e-commerce firms operating in India, which of the following statements is/are correct? 1. They can sell their own goods in addition to offering their platforms as market-places. 2. The degree to which they can own big sellers on their platforms is limited. [cite_start]Select the correct answer using the code given below: [cite: 134]
answer_explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect; under India's FDI policy, foreign-owned e-commerce entities are restricted to a pure marketplace model and are strictly prohibited from holding and selling their own inventory. Statement 2 is correct; FDI norms mandate that marketplace entities cannot hold direct equity in the sellers operating on their platform to prevent market dominance.
answer_sources: DPIIT Press Note 3 (2016) on FDI Policy in E-commerce
FEMA (Non-Debt Instruments) Rules
subject: Economy

question_text_combined: Which of the following activities constitute real sector in the economy? 1. [cite_start]Farmers harvesting their crops 2. Textile mills converting raw cotton into fabrics 3. A commercial bank lending money to a trading company 4. A corporate body issuing Rupee Denominated Bonds overseas [cite: 101]
answer_explanation: The real sector involves the actual production of goods and non-financial services. Activity 1 (agriculture) and Activity 2 (manufacturing) directly generate physical output and thus form the real sector. Activities 3 and 4 strictly belong to the financial sector, as they deal with capital intermediation and debt issuance.
answer_sources: NCERT Class 12 Macroeconomics: National Income Accounting
subject: Economy

question_text_combined: Which one of the following situations best reflects "Indirect Transfers" often talked about in media recently with reference to India? [cite: 133]
options_combined: An Indian company investing in a foreign enterprise and paying taxes to the foreign country on the profits arising out of its investment
A foreign company investing in India and paying taxes to the country of its base on the profits arising out of its investment
An Indian company purchases tangible assets in a foreign country and sells such assets after their value increases and transfers the proceeds to India
A foreign company transfers shares and such shares derive their substantial value from assets located in India
answer_explanation: Indirect transfer occurs when a foreign entity transfers shares or interest in a foreign company, but the value of those shares is substantially derived from assets physically located in India. This concept gained prominence during the Vodafone taxation dispute, leading to retrospective tax amendments by the Indian government.
answer_sources: Income Tax Act, 1961 (Section 9(1)(i) specific to Indirect Transfers)
Supreme Court Judgment in Vodafone International Holdings BV vs Union of India (2012)
subject: Economy

question_text_combined: With reference to the expenditure made by an organisation or a company, which of the following statements is/are correct? 1. Acquiring new technology is capital expenditure. 2. [cite_start]Debt financing is considered capital expenditure, while equity financing is considered revenue expenditure. [cite: 201]
answer_explanation: Statement 1 is correct; acquiring new technology creates an intangible asset that provides long-term economic benefits, thereby qualifying as capital expenditure. Statement 2 is fundamentally flawed; debt and equity financing are both sources of capital/funds, whereas expenditure classifies the 'use' of funds. Neither is revenue expenditure.
answer_sources: ICAI Accounting Standards (AS 26 Intangible Assets)
subject: Economy

question_text_combined: With reference to the Indian economy, consider the following statements: 1. A share of the household financial savings goes towards government borrowings. 2. Dated securities issued at market-related rates in auctions form a large component of internal debt. [cite_start]Which of the above statements is/are correct? [cite: 201]
answer_explanation: Statement 1 is correct; household financial savings channelled into bank deposits and small savings schemes are heavily invested by banks in government securities to meet SLR requirements and directly fund the fiscal deficit. Statement 2 is correct; the largest component of India's internal public debt consists of market borrowings through dated securities.
answer_sources: Ministry of Finance: Public Debt Management Cell (PDMC) Reports
RBI Bulletin on Savings and Borrowings
subject: Economy

question_text_combined: Consider the following statements: 1. Pursuant to the report of H.N. Sanyal Committee, the Contempt of Courts Act, 1971 was passed. 2. The Constitution of India empowers the Supreme Court and the High Courts to punish for contempt of themselves. 3. The Constitution of India defines Civil Contempt and Criminal Contempt. 4. In India, the Parliament is vested with the powers to make laws on Contempt of Court. [cite_start]Which of the statements given above is/are correct? [cite: 201]
answer_explanation: Statement 1 is correct; the 1971 Act was enacted based on the Sanyal Committee's recommendations. Statement 2 is correct; Articles 129 and 215 grant the SC and HCs respectively the status of a Court of Record and the power to punish for contempt. Statement 3 is incorrect; the Constitution does not define these terms; they are defined in Section 2 of the Contempt of Courts Act, 1971. Statement 4 is correct; Parliament has legislative competence to enact laws on contempt (Article 246 read with Entry 77 of List I and Entry 14 of List III).
answer_sources: Constitution of India (Articles 129, 215)
Contempt of Courts Act, 1971
subject: Polity

question_text_combined: With reference to India, consider the following statements: 1. Government law officers and legal firms are recognised as advocates, but corporate lawyers and patent attorneys are excluded from recognition as advocates. 2. Bar Councils have the power to lay down the rules relating to legal education and recognition of law colleges. [cite_start]Which of the statements given above is/are correct? [cite: 201]
answer_explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect; under the Advocates Act, 1961, only natural persons can enroll as advocates, completely excluding "legal firms" from being recognized as advocates. Furthermore, full-time salaried corporate lawyers are generally suspended from practice. Statement 2 is correct; the Bar Council of India regulates legal education and sets standards for recognizing law degrees and colleges under the Advocates Act.
answer_sources: Advocates Act, 1961
Bar Council of India Rules
subject: Polity

question_text_combined: Consider the following statements: 1. A bill amending the Constitution requires a prior recommendation of the President of India. 2. When a Constitution Amendment Bill is presented to the President of India, it is obligatory for the President of India to give his/her assent. 3. A Constitution Amendment Bill must be passed by both the Lok Sabha and the Rajya Sabha by a special majority and there is no provision for joint sitting. [cite_start]Which of the statements given above are correct? [cite: 205]
answer_explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect; Article 368 does not require prior presidential recommendation to introduce a Constitutional Amendment Bill. Statement 2 is correct; the 24th Amendment Act (1971) made it mandatory for the President to give assent to such bills. Statement 3 is correct; each House must pass the bill independently by a special majority, and the provision for a joint sitting (Article 108) explicitly does not apply to amendment bills.
answer_sources: Constitution of India (Article 368, Article 108)
subject: Polity

question_text_combined: Consider the following statements: 1. The Constitution of India classifies the ministers into four ranks viz. Cabinet Minister, Minister of State with Independent Charge, Minister of State and Deputy Minister. 2. The total number of ministers in the Union Government, including the Prime Minister, shall not exceed 15 percent of the total number of members in the Lok Sabha. [cite_start]Which of the statements given above is/are correct? [cite: 205]
answer_explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect; the Constitution does not classify ministers into four ranks. It only mentions the 'Council of Ministers' and the 'Cabinet' (under Article 352). The ranking is a British parliamentary convention formalized by Rules of Business. Statement 2 is correct; Article 75(1A), introduced by the 91st Amendment Act, caps the total size of the Council of Ministers at 15% of the total strength of the Lok Sabha.
answer_sources: Constitution of India (Article 74, 75(1A), 352)
subject: Polity

question_text_combined: Which of the following is/are the exclusive power(s) of Lok Sabha? 1. [cite_start]To ratify the declaration of Emergency 2. To pass a motion of no-confidence against the Council of Ministers 3. To impeach the President of India [cite: 205]
answer_explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect; a proclamation of National Emergency (Article 352) must be ratified by both Houses of Parliament. Statement 2 is correct; under Article 75(3), the Council of Ministers is collectively responsible solely to the House of the People (Lok Sabha), meaning only Lok Sabha can pass a no-confidence motion. Statement 3 is incorrect; impeachment of the President (Article 61) can be initiated and passed by either House.
answer_sources: Constitution of India (Articles 61, 75(3), 352)
subject: Polity

question_text_combined: With reference to anti-defection law in India, consider the following statements: 1. The law specifies that a nominated legislator cannot join any political party within six months of being appointed to the House. 2. The law does not provide any time-frame within which the presiding officer has to decide a defection case. [cite_start]Which of the statements given above is/are correct? [cite: 205]
answer_explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect; paragraph 2(3) of the Tenth Schedule states that a nominated member is disqualified if they join a political party *after* the expiry of six months. This explicitly means they are permitted to join a party *within* the first six months. Statement 2 is correct; the Tenth Schedule prescribes no fixed time limit for the Presiding Officer to adjudicate on disqualification petitions.
answer_sources: Constitution of India (Tenth Schedule)
subject: Polity

question_text_combined: Consider the following statements: 1. Attorney General of India and Solicitor General of India are the only officers of the Government who are allowed to participate in the meetings of the Parliament of India. 2. According to the Constitution of India, the Attorney General of India submits his resignation when the Government which appointed him resigns. [cite_start]Which of the statements given above is/are correct? [cite: 253]
answer_explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect; under Article 88, only the Attorney General holds the constitutional right to speak and participate in parliamentary proceedings. The Solicitor General is a statutory role without this constitutional privilege. Statement 2 is incorrect; while it is a strong convention for the AG to resign when the ruling government changes, the Constitution itself (Article 76(4)) strictly states only that the AG holds office during the pleasure of the President, not that resignation is constitutionally mandated upon the government's resignation.
answer_sources: Constitution of India (Article 76, 88)
subject: Polity

question_text_combined: With reference to the writs issued by the Courts in India, consider the following statements: 1. Mandamus will not lie against a private organisation unless it is entrusted with a public duty. 2. Mandamus will not lie against a Company even though it may be a Government Company. 3. Any public minded person can be a petitioner to move the Court to obtain the writ of Quo Warranto. [cite_start]Which of the statements given above are correct? [cite: 278]
answer_explanation: Statement 1 is correct; the writ of Mandamus lies to compel the performance of a public or statutory duty, even if the body is private but acting in a public capacity. Statement 2 is incorrect; a Government Company qualifies as 'State' under Article 12, making it wholly subject to Mandamus. Statement 3 is correct; the writ of Quo Warranto acts as an exception to the strict locus standi rule, allowing any interested, public-minded person to challenge the unlawful usurpation of a public office.
answer_sources: Constitution of India (Articles 32, 226)
Supreme Court judgments on Locus Standi and Writs
subject: Polity

question_text_combined: With reference to Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission, consider the following statements: 1. Private and public hospitals must adopt it. 2. As it aims to achieve universal health coverage, every citizen of India should be part of it ultimately. 3. It has seamless portability across the country. [cite_start]Which of the statements given above is/are correct? [cite: 258]
answer_explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect; participation and integration with the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) is voluntary for healthcare facilities. Statement 2 is incorrect; creating an Ayushman Bharat Health Account (ABHA) is completely voluntary for citizens. Statement 3 is correct; one of the core features of ABDM is facilitating seamless portability of digital health records across service providers pan-India.
answer_sources: National Health Authority (NHA): Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission Framework
subject: Polity

question_text_combined: With reference to Deputy Speaker of Lok Sabha, consider the following statements: 1. As per the Rules of Procedure and Conduct of Business in Lok Sabha, the election of Deputy Speaker shall be held on such date as the Speaker may fix. 2. There is a mandatory provision that the election of a candidate as Deputy Speaker of Lok Sabha shall be from either the principal opposition party or the ruling party. 3. The Deputy Speaker has the same power as of the Speaker when presiding over the sitting of the House and no appeal lies against his rulings. 4. The well established parliamentary practice regarding the appointment of Deputy Speaker is that the motion is moved by the Speaker and duly seconded by the Prime Minister. [cite_start]Which of the statements given above are correct? [cite: 286]
answer_explanation: Statement 1 is correct; under Rule 8 of the Rules of Procedure of the Lok Sabha, the Speaker fixes the date for the Deputy Speaker's election. Statement 2 is incorrect; while it is a parliamentary convention to offer the post to the opposition, there is no mandatory constitutional or statutory provision. Statement 3 is correct; the Deputy Speaker acts with full authority of the Speaker when presiding, and rulings are final. Statement 4 is incorrect; there is no established practice that the Speaker moves the motion.
answer_sources: Rules of Procedure and Conduct of Business in Lok Sabha (Rule 8)
Constitution of India (Article 93, 95)
subject: Polity

question_text_combined: Among the following crops, which one is the most important anthropogenic source of both methane and nitrous oxide ? [cite: 394]
answer_explanation: Flooded rice paddies are a massive global anthropogenic source of methane due to the anaerobic decomposition of organic matter in the soil. Additionally, the intensive application of nitrogenous fertilizers in rice cultivation makes it a major source of nitrous oxide emissions.
answer_sources: IPCC Reports on Agriculture, Forestry and Other Land Use (AFOLU)
FAO Reports on Rice Cultivation and Greenhouse Gas Emissions
subject: Environment

question_text_combined: "System of Rice Intensification" of cultivation, in which alternate wetting and drying of rice fields is practised, results in: 1. Reduced seed requirement 2. Reduced methane production 3. Reduced electricity consumption Select the correct answer using the code given below: [cite: 404]
answer_explanation: Statement 1 is correct; the System of Rice Intensification (SRI) uses younger, single seedlings planted at wider spacing, drastically reducing the seed requirement. Statement 2 is correct; intermittent wetting and drying prevents continuous anaerobic conditions, thereby significantly lowering methane emissions. Statement 3 is correct; reduced continuous flooding means less groundwater pumping is required, saving electricity and fuel.
answer_sources: ICAR publications on System of Rice Intensification (SRI)
subject: Environment

question_text_combined: Which one of the following lakes of West Africa has become dry and turned into a desert? [cite: 445]
answer_explanation: Lake Faguibine in Mali, West Africa, suffered severely from prolonged droughts in the 1970s and 1980s. The once-massive lake dried up almost completely, transforming into a dry, desert-like environment, crippling local livelihoods.
answer_sources: UNEP Reports on Lake Faguibine drying up
subject: Geography

question_text_combined: Gandikota canyon of South India was created by which one of the following rivers? [cite: 465]
answer_explanation: The Gandikota canyon, often referred to as the Grand Canyon of India, is a spectacular gorge formed by the river Pennar cutting through the Erramala hills in Andhra Pradesh.
answer_sources: Andhra Pradesh Tourism Documentation
subject: Geography

question_text_combined: Consider the following pairs: Peak - Mountains 1. Namcha Barwa - Garhwal Himalaya 2. Nanda Devi - Kumaon Himalaya 3. Nokrek - Sikkim Himalaya Which of the pairs given above is/are correctly matched? [cite: 405]
answer_explanation: Pair 1 is incorrect; Namcha Barwa is located in the Eastern Himalayas near the Tibet-Arunachal Pradesh border, not the Garhwal Himalayas. Pair 2 is correct; Nanda Devi is prominently located in the Kumaon division of Uttarakhand. Pair 3 is incorrect; Nokrek is the highest peak in the Garo Hills in Meghalaya, completely separate from the Sikkim Himalayas.
answer_sources: NCERT Class 11 India Physical Environment
subject: Geography

question_text_combined: The term "Levant" often heard in the news roughly corresponds to which of the following regions?
options_combined: Region along the eastern Mediterranean shores
Region along North African shores stretching from Egypt to Morocco
Region along Persian Gulf and Horn of Africa
The entire coastal areas of Mediterranean Sea
answer_explanation: The Levant is an approximate historical and geographical term referring to a large area in the Eastern Mediterranean region, covering countries like Syria, Lebanon, Jordan, Israel, Palestine, and Cyprus.
answer_sources: Britannica: Levant
subject: Geography

question_text_combined: Consider the following countries: 1. Azerbaijan 2. Kyrgyzstan 3. Tajikistan 4. Turkmenistan 5. Uzbekistan. Which of the above have borders with Afghanistan?
answer_explanation: Afghanistan shares its borders with Pakistan to the east and south, Iran to the west, Turkmenistan, Uzbekistan, and Tajikistan to the north, and China to the northeast. Azerbaijan and Kyrgyzstan do not share a border with Afghanistan.
answer_sources: Oxford Atlas: Political Map of Central and South Asia
subject: Geography

question_text_combined: With reference to India, consider the following statements: 1. Monazite is a source of rare earths. 2. Monazite contains thorium. 3. Monazite occurs naturally in the entire Indian coastal sands in India. 4. In India, Government bodies only can process or export monazite. Which of the statements given above are correct?
answer_explanation: Statement 1 and 2 are correct; Monazite is a primary ore of several rare earth elements and contains thorium. Statement 3 is incorrect; it is not found across the 'entire' coast, but in concentrated deposits in states like Kerala, Tamil Nadu, Andhra Pradesh, and Odisha. Statement 4 is correct; Monazite is a prescribed substance under the Atomic Energy Act, and its processing/export is restricted to wholly-owned government entities like Indian Rare Earths Limited (IREL).
answer_sources: Department of Atomic Energy (DAE) guidelines on Prescribed Substances
Indian Bureau of Mines (IBM) Minerals Yearbook
subject: Geography

question_text_combined: In the northern hemisphere, the longest day of the year normally occurs in the:
options_combined: First half of the month of June
Second half of the month of June
First half of the month of July
Second half of the month of July
answer_explanation: The longest day of the year in the northern hemisphere is the summer solstice, which typically occurs on or around June 21st, placing it in the second half of the month of June.
answer_sources: NCERT Class 6 Geography: Motions of the Earth
subject: Geography

question_text_combined: Consider the following pairs: Wetland/Lake - Location 1. Hokera Wetland - Punjab 2. Renuka Wetland - Himachal Pradesh 3. Rudrasagar Lake - Tripura 4. Sasthamkotta Lake - Tamil Nadu. How many pairs given above are correctly matched?
answer_explanation: Only two pairs are correctly matched. Hokera Wetland is located in Jammu & Kashmir, not Punjab (Pair 1 incorrect). Renuka Wetland is in Himachal Pradesh (Pair 2 correct). Rudrasagar Lake is in Tripura (Pair 3 correct). Sasthamkotta Lake is in Kerala, not Tamil Nadu (Pair 4 incorrect).
answer_sources: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change: Ramsar Sites in India
subject: Environment

question_text_combined: Consider the following: 1. Aarogya Setu 2. COWIN 3. DigiLocker 4. DIKSHA. Which of the above are built on top of open-source digital platforms?
answer_explanation: The Government of India has a policy to build digital public goods using open-source platforms. Aarogya Setu, CoWIN, DigiLocker, and DIKSHA (Digital Infrastructure for Knowledge Sharing) are all built on top of open APIs and open-source architectures.
answer_sources: Ministry of Electronics and IT (MeitY): National Open Digital Ecosystems (NODE) Policy
subject: Science & Tech

question_text_combined: With reference to Web 3-0, consider the following statements: 1. Web 3-0 technology enables people to control their own data. 2. In Web 3-0 world, there can be blockchain based social networks. 3. Web 3-0 is operated by users collectively rather than a corporation. Which of the statements given above are correct?
answer_explanation: Web 3.0 is characterized by decentralization, token-based economics, and blockchain technology. Statement 1 is correct as it shifts ownership back to users. Statement 2 is correct as decentralized apps (dApps) include blockchain-based social networks. Statement 3 is correct as network governance is distributed among users collectively instead of centralized tech giants.
answer_sources: Ethereum Foundation: Web3 Fundamentals
subject: Science & Tech

question_text_combined: With reference to "Software as a Service (SaaS)", consider the following statements: 1. SaaS buyers can customise the user interface and can change data fields. 2. SaaS users can access their data through their mobile devices. 3. Outlook, Hotmail and Yahoo! Mail are forms of SaaS. Which of the statements given above are correct?
answer_explanation: SaaS is a software distribution model where a cloud provider hosts applications. Statement 1 is correct; modern SaaS applications offer deep customization in UI and data parameters. Statement 2 is correct; cloud-based SaaS is naturally multi-platform and accessible via mobile devices. Statement 3 is correct; web-based email providers like Outlook and Yahoo! Mail are classic, consumer-facing examples of SaaS.
answer_sources: NIST Cloud Computing Definition (Special Publication 800-145)
subject: Science & Tech

question_text_combined: Which one of the following statements best reflects the idea behind the "Fractional Orbital Bombardment System" often talked about in media?
options_combined: A hypersonic missile is launched into space to counter the asteroid approaching the Earth and explode it in space.
A spacecraft lands on another planet after making several orbital motions.
A missile is put into a stable orbit around the Earth and deorbits over a target on the Earth.
A spacecraft moves along a comet with the same speed and places a probe on its surface.
answer_explanation: The Fractional Orbital Bombardment System (FOBS) is a method of delivering a nuclear warhead wherein the vehicle is placed into a low Earth orbit, meaning it can attack from any direction, bypassing traditional early warning radars. It deorbits over its target before completing a full revolution.
answer_sources: Nuclear Threat Initiative (NTI) Reports on FOBS
subject: Science & Tech

question_text_combined: Which one of the following is the context in which the term "qubit" is mentioned?
answer_explanation: A qubit, or quantum bit, is the basic unit of quantum information in quantum computing, representing a two-state quantum-mechanical system capable of existing in superposition.
answer_sources: IBM Quantum Computing Glossary
subject: Science & Tech

question_text_combined: Consider the following communication technologies: 1. Closed-circuit Television 2. Radio Frequency Identification 3. Wireless Local Area Network. Which of the above are considered Short-Range devices/technologies?
answer_explanation: All three are localized communication technologies. CCTV functions within a closed, localized circuit (often within a single building). RFID is explicitly designed for short-range communication (centimeters to a few meters). WLAN (like Wi-Fi) provides localized wireless networking within limited areas like homes or offices.
answer_sources: Ministry of Communications (India): National Frequency Allocation Plan (NFAP)
subject: Science & Tech

question_text_combined: Consider the following statements: 1. Biofilms can form on medical implants within human tissues. 2. Biofilms can form on food and food processing surfaces. 3. Biofilms can exhibit antibiotic resistance. Which of the statements given above are correct?
answer_explanation: Biofilms are complex, matrix-enclosed communities of microorganisms. Statement 1 is correct; they commonly form on catheters and medical implants. Statement 2 is correct; they are a major concern in the food processing industry as they adhere to surfaces. Statement 3 is correct; the extracellular matrix in biofilms inherently provides massive resistance to antimicrobial agents, including antibiotics.
answer_sources: NCBI/NIH Literature on Microbial Biofilms
subject: Science & Tech

question_text_combined: Consider the following statements in respect of probiotics: 1. Probiotics are made of both bacteria and yeast. 2. The organisms in probiotics are found in foods we ingest but they do not naturally occur in our gut. 3. Probiotics help in the digestion of milk sugars. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
answer_explanation: Statement 1 is correct; commercial probiotics contain beneficial strains of both bacteria (like Lactobacillus) and yeast (like Saccharomyces boulardii). Statement 2 is completely incorrect; the very definition of a probiotic strain requires that it is analogous or identical to the beneficial microorganisms naturally found in the human gut biome. Statement 3 is correct; probiotic bacteria produce lactase, which aids in digesting lactose (milk sugars).
answer_sources: WHO/FAO Guidelines on Probiotics in Food
subject: Science & Tech

question_text_combined: In the context of vaccines manufactured to prevent COVID-19 pandemic, consider the following statements: 1. The Serum Institute of India produced COVID-19 vaccine named Covishield using mRNA platform. 2. Sputnik V vaccine is manufactured using vector based platform. 3. COVAXIN is an inactivated pathogen based vaccine. Which of the statements given above are correct?
answer_explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect; Covishield is a viral vector vaccine (using a modified chimpanzee adenovirus), not an mRNA vaccine (like Pfizer/Moderna). Statement 2 is correct; Sputnik V relies on an adenoviral vector platform. Statement 3 is correct; COVAXIN, developed by Bharat Biotech, uses a Whole-Virion Inactivated Vero Cell derived platform technology.
answer_sources: WHO COVID-19 Vaccine Tracker
Ministry of Health and Family Welfare (MoHFW) COVID Vaccine documentation
subject: Science & Tech

question_text_combined: If a major solar storm (solar flare) reaches the Earth, which of the following are the possible effects on the Earth? 1. GPS and navigation systems could fail. 2. Tsunamis could occur at equatorial regions. 3. Power grids could be damaged. 4. Intense auroras could occur over much of the Earth. 5. Forest fires could take place over much of the planet. 6. Orbits of the satellites could be disturbed. 7. Shortwave radio communication of the aircraft flying over polar regions could be interrupted.
answer_explanation: Solar storms cause geomagnetic disturbances. They can severely disrupt GPS/navigation (1), damage power grid transformers (3), produce intense auroras (4), increase atmospheric drag disturbing satellite orbits (6), and blackout high-frequency polar radio comms (7). However, they do not trigger terrestrial tectonic events like Tsunamis (2) nor do they directly ignite forest fires across the planet (5).
answer_sources: NOAA Space Weather Prediction Center
subject: Science & Tech

question_text_combined: "Climate Action Tracker" which monitors the emission reduction pledges of different countries is a :
options_combined: Database created by coalition of research organisations
Wing of "International Panel of Climate Change"
Committee under "United Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change"
Agency promoted and financed by United Nations Environment Programme and World Bank
answer_explanation: The Climate Action Tracker (CAT) is an independent scientific analysis produced by a consortium of two research organizations: Climate Analytics and NewClimate Institute. It is not an official UN, IPCC, or World Bank wing.
answer_sources: Climate Action Tracker Official Website
subject: Environment

question_text_combined: Consider the following statements: 1. "The Climate Group" is an international non-profit organization that drives climate action by building large networks and runs them. 2. The International Energy Agency in partnership with the Climate Group launched a global initiative "EP100". 3. EP100 brings together leading companies committed to driving innovation in energy efficiency and increasing competitiveness while delivering on emission reduction goals. 4. Some Indian companies are members of EP100. 5. The International Energy Agency is the Secretariat to the "Under2 Coalition". Which of the statements given above are correct?
answer_explanation: Statement 1 is correct; The Climate Group is an international NGO focusing on climate networks. Statement 3 is correct regarding EP100's energy efficiency goal. Statement 4 is correct (e.g., Mahindra & Mahindra is a member). Statement 2 is incorrect; EP100 was launched by The Climate Group in partnership with the Alliance to Save Energy, not the IEA. Statement 5 is incorrect; The Climate Group acts as the secretariat to the Under2 Coalition, not the IEA.
answer_sources: The Climate Group: EP100 Initiative
Under2 Coalition Official Secretariat Structure
subject: Environment

question_text_combined: "If rainforests and tropical forests are the lungs of the Earth, then surely wetlands function as its kidneys." Which one of the following functions of wetlands best reflects the above statement?
options_combined: The water cycle in wetlands involves surface runoff, subsoil percolation and evaporation.
Algae form the nutrient base upon which fish, crustaceans, molluscs, birds, reptiles and mammals thrive.
Wetlands play a vital role in maintaining sedimentation balance and soil stabilization.
Aquatic plants absorb heavy metals and excess nutrients.
answer_explanation: Kidneys serve the biological function of filtering waste and toxins from the blood. The ecosystem analogy maps strictly to how wetlands purify water. Specifically, the aquatic flora in wetlands extract and absorb heavy metals, agricultural runoff, and excess nutrients, effectively filtering the water exactly as kidneys filter blood.
answer_sources: Ramsar Convention Reports on Wetland Ecosystem Services
subject: Environment

question_text_combined: In the context of WHO Air Quality Guidelines, consider the following statements: 1. The 24-hour mean of PM2.5 should not exceed 15 ug/m3 and annual mean should not exceed 5 ug/m3. 2. In a year, the highest levels of ozone pollution occur during the periods of inclement weather. 3. PM10 can penetrate the lung barrier and enter the bloodstream. 4. Excessive ozone in the air can trigger asthma. Which of the statements given above are correct?
answer_explanation: Statement 1 aligns with the updated 2021 WHO Global Air Quality Guidelines. Statement 2 is incorrect; surface ozone levels peak during hot, sunny, and clear weather due to photochemical reactions, not inclement weather. Statement 3 is incorrect; while PM10 enters the lungs, it is the much finer PM2.5 (and smaller) particles that cross the lung barrier into the bloodstream. Statement 4 is correct; ozone is a severe respiratory irritant that triggers asthma.
answer_sources: WHO Global Air Quality Guidelines 2021
subject: Environment

question_text_combined: With reference to "Gucchi" sometimes mentioned in the news, consider the following statements: 1. It is a fungus. 2. It grows in some Himalayan forest areas. 3. It is commercially cultivated in the Himalayan foothills of north-eastern India. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
answer_explanation: Statement 1 is correct; Gucchi (Morchella esculenta) is a species of edible fungus (morel mushroom). Statement 2 is correct; it grows wild in the conifer forests of the Northwestern Himalayas (like Jammu & Kashmir and Himachal Pradesh). Statement 3 is incorrect; it is a wild, highly unpredictable crop that defies commercial cultivation and is not commercially farmed in North-Eastern India.
answer_sources: ICAR documentation on Wild Edible Mushrooms of India
subject: Environment

question_text_combined: With reference to polyethylene terephthalate, the use of which is so widespread in our daily lives, consider the following statements: 1. Its fibres can be blended with wool and cotton fibres to reinforce their properties. 2. Containers made of it can be used to store any alcoholic beverage. 3. Bottles made of it can be recycled into other products. 4. Articles made of it can be easily disposed of by incineration without causing greenhouse gas emissions. Which of the statements given above are correct?
answer_explanation: Statement 1 is correct; PET fibers (polyester) are commonly blended with cotton (forming polycotton) and wool. Statement 3 is correct; PET is highly recyclable (into clothing, new bottles, etc.). Statement 4 is absurdly incorrect; incinerating PET plastics releases significant greenhouse gases, especially CO2. (Statement 2's use of 'any' is too broad, but eliminating 4 leaves '1 and 3' as the only viable option).
answer_sources: UNEP reports on Plastics and Climate Change
subject: Science & Tech
assumptions: Options rule out Statement 2 by process of elimination due to the definitive falsity of Statement 4.

question_text_combined: Which of the following is not a bird?
answer_explanation: The Golden Mahseer is a well-known, large freshwater fish found in the Himalayan rivers. The Indian Nightjar, Spoonbill, and White Ibis are all species of birds.
answer_sources: Zoological Survey of India (ZSI): Fauna of India
subject: Environment

question_text_combined: Which of the following are nitrogen-fixing plants? 1. Alfalfa 2. Amaranth 3. Chickpea 4. Clover 5. Purslane (Kulfa) 6. Spinach. Select the correct answer using the code given below:
answer_explanation: Nitrogen fixation typically occurs via a symbiotic relationship between Rhizobium bacteria and the root nodules of leguminous plants. Alfalfa (1), Chickpea (3), and Clover (4) are legumes and possess this capability. Amaranth, Purslane, and Spinach belong to non-leguminous plant families and are not recognized as nitrogen fixers.
answer_sources: NCERT Class 11 Biology: Mineral Nutrition
subject: Environment

question_text_combined: "Biorock technology" is talked about in which one of the following situations?
options_combined: Development of building materials using plant residues
Identification of areas for exploration/extraction of shale gas
Providing salt licks for wild animals in forests/protected areas
answer_explanation: Biorock technology (also known as Seacrete) involves passing a low-voltage electrical current through seawater via a steel structure. This causes dissolved minerals to crystallize and form a limestone-like crust, which provides an ideal substrate to accelerate the growth and restoration of damaged coral reefs.
answer_sources: Zoological Survey of India (ZSI) initiatives in the Gulf of Kachchh
subject: Environment

question_text_combined: The "Miyawaki method" is well known for the:
options_combined: Promotion of commercial farming in arid and semi-arid areas
Development of gardens using genetically modified flora
Creation of mini forests in urban areas
Harvesting wind energy on coastal areas and on sea surfaces
answer_explanation: Pioneered by Japanese botanist Akira Miyawaki, this method involves planting dozens of native species closely together to drastically accelerate growth. It is highly popular for creating dense, fast-growing, biodiverse 'mini forests' in small, degraded, or urban spaces.
answer_sources: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change: Urban Forestry Initiatives
subject: Environment

question_text_combined: In the Government of India Act 1919, the functions of Provincial Government were divided into
and
subjects. Which of the following were treated as
subjects? 1. Administration of Justice 2. Local Self-Government 3. Land Revenue 4. Police
answer_explanation: Under the dyarchy system introduced by the Government of India Act 1919, subjects were divided. 'Reserved' subjects included crucial areas like Police, Justice, Land Revenue, and Irrigation, administered by the Governor and his executive council. 'Transferred' subjects included Local Self-Government, Education, and Health, administered by ministers.
answer_sources: Bipan Chandra: India's Struggle for Independence
subject: History

question_text_combined: In medieval India, the term
referred to:
answer_explanation: Fanam (or Panam) was a small coin used in medieval Southern India, particularly in regions like Travancore, Madras, and the Vijayanagara Empire. They were minted in gold and silver.
answer_sources: NCERT Class 12 History: Themes in Indian History Part II
subject: History

question_text_combined: Consider the following freedom fighters: 1. Barindra Kumar Ghosh 2. Jogesh Chandra Chatterjee 3. Rash Behari Bose. Who of the above was/were actively associated with the Ghadar Party?
answer_explanation: Rash Behari Bose was actively associated with the Ghadar movement and was instrumental in organizing the failed Ghadar mutiny of 1915. Barindra Kumar Ghosh was associated with the Anushilan Samiti and Jugantar. Jogesh Chandra Chatterjee was a founding member of the Hindustan Republican Association (HRA).
answer_sources: Bipan Chandra: India's Struggle for Independence
subject: History

question_text_combined: With reference to the proposals of Cripps Mission, consider the following statements: 1. The Constituent Assembly would have members nominated by the Provincial Assemblies as well as the Princely States. 2. Any Province, which is not prepared to accept the new Constitution would have the right to sign a separate agreement with Britain regarding its future status. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
answer_explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect; the Cripps Mission proposed that members from the provinces would be elected by the lower houses of the provincial legislatures (not nominated), while members from Princely States would be nominated by the rulers. Statement 2 is correct; it explicitly allowed provinces unwilling to join the Indian Union to form their own separate union and sign a separate agreement with Britain.
answer_sources: Spectrum Modern Indian History: Cripps Mission Proposals
subject: History

question_text_combined: With reference to Indian history, consider the following texts: 1. Nettipakarana 2. Parishishtaparvan 3. Avadanashataka 4. Trishashtilakshana Mahapurana. Which of the above are Jaina texts?
answer_explanation: Parishishtaparvan (by Hemachandra) and Trishashtilakshana Mahapurana (by Jinasena) are significant Jaina texts. Nettipakarana (a Pali text) and Avadanashataka (a Sanskrit text) belong to the Buddhist tradition.
answer_sources: Upinder Singh: A History of Ancient and Early Medieval India
subject: History

question_text_combined: With reference to Indian history, consider the following pairs: Historical person - Known as 1. Aryadeva - Jaina scholar 2. Dignaga - Buddhist scholar 3. Nathamuni - Vaishnava scholar. How many pairs given above are correctly matched?
answer_explanation: Pair 1 is incorrect; Aryadeva was a prominent 3rd-century Mahayana Buddhist monk and disciple of Nagarjuna. Pair 2 is correct; Dignaga was a famous Buddhist scholar and founder of Indian logic (Pramana). Pair 3 is correct; Nathamuni was a famous Vaishnava theologian who collected the Nalayira Divya Prabandham.
answer_sources: Upinder Singh: A History of Ancient and Early Medieval India
subject: History

question_text_combined: With reference to Indian history, consider the following statements: 1. The first Mongol invasion of India happened during the reign of Jalal-ud-din Khalji. 2. During the reign of Ala-ud-din Khalji, one Mongol assault marched up to Delhi and besieged the city. 3. Muhammad-bin-Tughlaq temporarily lost portions of north-west of his kingdom to Mongols. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
answer_explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect; the first major Mongol invasion occurred in 1241 under Bahram Shah, long before Jalal-ud-din Khalji. Genghis Khan also reached the Indus during Iltutmish's reign. Statement 2 is correct; Targhi's invasion during Ala-ud-din Khalji's reign saw Mongols besiege Delhi (Siri). Statement 3 is often debated but broadly incorrect as Tughlaq defeated Tarmashirin, though some historical texts suggest he bought them off or temporarily ceded minor frontier control, making the best elimination answer option B (if we only consider statement 2 as absolutely correct, though UPSC key usually aligns with this pattern). Wait, statement 3 states he temporarily lost portions, while historically Alauddin lost nothing, Muhammad bin Tughlaq's empire faced a huge invasion but didn't lose vast territory. The official key marks statement 2 alone as correct in some interpretations, but based on options, '2 only' was an option. Correction: Options are 1 and 2, 2 and 3, 1 and 3, 3 only. Looking closely at the question image, the options provided in the JSON are derived from the Hindi text for question 57 (a=1 and 2, b=2 only, c=1 and 3, d=3 only). The English text says (a) 1 and 2 only, (b) 2 and 3 only, (c) 1 and 3 only, (d) 1, 2 and 3. Wait, looking at the image provided for English Q57 options: (a) 1 and 2 only, (b) 2 and 3 only, (c) 1 and 3 only, (d) 1, 2 and 3. Actually, the answer is just 2, but '2 only' is not in English options. Let's re-verify. Hindi options: (a) 1 & 2, (b) keval 2, (c) 1 & 3, (d) keval 3. Wait! Option (b) in English is missing in the crop, but looking at standard papers, option (b) is '2 only'. I will assume '2 only' is the correct choice.
answer_sources: Satish Chandra: Medieval India
subject: History
assumptions: The English options in the OCR text had a slight misalignment; the correct option corresponding to '2 only' is chosen.

question_text_combined: With reference to Indian history, who of the following were known as
answer_explanation: During the Delhi Sultanate period, the Sayyids (who claimed descent from the Prophet) put on a pointed cap known as 'kulah' and were therefore referred to as 'Kulah-Daran'.
answer_sources: Satish Chandra: Medieval India
subject: History

question_text_combined: With reference to Indian history, consider the following statements: 1. The Dutch established their factories/warehouses on the east coast on lands granted to them by Gajapati rulers. 2. Alfonso de Albuquerque captured Goa from the Bijapur Sultanate. 3. The English East India Company established a factory at Madras on a plot of land leased from a representative of the Vijayanagara empire. Which of the statements given above are correct?
answer_explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect; the Gajapati dynasty ruled Odisha from 1434 to 1541. The Dutch established their first factory on the east coast at Masulipatnam in 1605, long after the Gajapati empire fell. Statement 2 is correct; Alfonso de Albuquerque captured Goa from the Adil Shahi dynasty of Bijapur in 1510. Statement 3 is correct; the British leased Madras in 1639 from Damarla Venkatadri Nayaka, a feudatory of the declining Vijayanagara Empire (Chandragiri).
answer_sources: Spectrum Modern Indian History: Advent of Europeans
subject: History

question_text_combined: According to Kautilya's Arthashastra, which of the following are correct? 1. A person could be a slave as a result of a judicial punishment. 2. If a female slave bore her master a son, she was legally free. 3. If a son born to a female slave was fathered by her master, the son was entitled to the legal status of the master's son. Which of the statements given above are correct?
answer_explanation: All three statements are correct per Kautilya's Arthashastra. It outlines several types of slavery, including enslavement for inability to pay fines (judicial punishment). It also explicitly states that if a master fathers a child with his female slave (dasi), both the mother and the child gain their freedom, and the child holds the legal status of the master's son.
answer_sources: Upinder Singh: A History of Ancient and Early Medieval India
subject: History
assumptions: Option parsing from raw text merges D to 1,2,3.

question_text_combined: Consider the following statements: 1. Tight monetary policy of US Federal Reserve could lead to capital flight. 2. Capital flight may increase the interest cost of firms with existing External Commercial Borrowings (ECBs). 3. Devaluation of domestic currency decreases the currency risk associated with ECBs. Which of the statements given above are correct?
answer_explanation: Statement 1 is correct; tightening by the US Fed increases US interest rates, leading foreign investors to pull capital out of emerging markets (capital flight). Statement 2 is correct; capital flight causes the domestic currency to depreciate, making it more expensive for domestic firms to service their foreign debt (ECBs). Statement 3 is incorrect; devaluation of the domestic currency increases the currency risk and repayment burden for unhedged ECBs.
answer_sources: NCERT Class 12 Macroeconomics
subject: Economy

question_text_combined: Consider the following States: 1. Andhra Pradesh 2. Kerala 3. Himachal Pradesh 4. Tripura. How many of the above are generally known as tea-producing States?
answer_explanation: Kerala, Himachal Pradesh (Kangra tea), and Tripura are well-known traditional tea-producing states. While Andhra Pradesh produces minimal tea experimentally in areas like Araku Valley, it is not 'generally known' as a major tea-producing state in the context of commercial geographic distribution. Thus, three states apply.
answer_sources: Tea Board of India: Tea Producing States
subject: Geography

question_text_combined: Consider the following statements: 1. In India, credit rating agencies are regulated by Reserve Bank of India. 2. The rating agency popularly known as ICRA is a public limited company. 3. Brickwork Ratings is an Indian credit rating agency. Which of the statements given above are correct?
answer_explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect; Credit Rating Agencies in India are regulated by the Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI) under the SEBI (Credit Rating Agencies) Regulations, 1999, not the RBI. Statement 2 is correct; ICRA is a publicly listed public limited company. Statement 3 is correct; Brickwork Ratings is a recognized Indian credit rating agency.
answer_sources: SEBI (Credit Rating Agencies) Regulations, 1999
subject: Economy

question_text_combined: With reference to the 'Banks Board Bureau (BBB)', which of the following statements are correct? 1. The Governor of RBI is the Chairman of BBB. 2. BBB recommends for the selection of heads for Public Sector Banks. 3. BBB helps the Public Sector Banks in developing strategies and capital raising plans. Select the correct answer using the code given below:
answer_explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect; the Banks Board Bureau (BBB) is headed by an independently appointed Chairman, not the RBI Governor. Statements 2 and 3 are correct; the BBB was formed specifically to recommend whole-time directors and non-executive chairpersons for PSBs, and to advise them on capital-raising strategies.
answer_sources: Ministry of Finance: Banks Board Bureau mandate
subject: Economy

question_text_combined: With reference to Convertible Bonds, consider the following statements: 1. As there is an option to exchange the bond for equity, Convertible Bonds pay a lower rate of interest. 2. The option to convert to equity affords the bondholder a degree of indexation to rising consumer prices. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
answer_explanation: Statement 1 is correct; investors accept a lower coupon rate (interest) on convertible bonds because they get the added value of the equity conversion option. Statement 2 is correct; since equity prices typically rise with inflation (as company earnings grow nominally), converting debt into equity provides a natural hedge (indexation) against rising consumer prices.
answer_sources: Investopedia: Convertible Bond
subject: Economy

question_text_combined: Consider the following: 1. Asian Infrastructure Investment Bank 2. Missile Technology Control Regime 3. Shanghai Cooperation Organisation. India is a member of which of the above?
answer_explanation: India is a founding member of the Asian Infrastructure Investment Bank (AIIB). It officially joined the Missile Technology Control Regime (MTCR) in 2016. It became a full member of the Shanghai Cooperation Organisation (SCO) in 2017. Therefore, India is a member of all three.
answer_sources: Ministry of External Affairs (MEA) Multilateral Organizations Database
subject: International Relations

question_text_combined: Consider the following statements: 1. Vietnam has been one of the fastest growing economies in recent years. 2. Vietnam is led by a multi-party political system. 3. Vietnam's economic growth is linked to its integration with global supply chains and focus on exports. 4. For a long time Vietnam's low labour costs and stable exchange rates have attracted global manufacturers. 5. Vietnam has the most productive e-service sector in the Indo-Pacific region. Which of the statements given above are correct?
answer_explanation: Statements 1, 3, and 4 correctly describe Vietnam's economic trajectory as an export-driven manufacturing hub with low labor costs. Statement 2 is incorrect; Vietnam is a one-party communist state led by the Communist Party of Vietnam. Statement 5 is incorrect; nations like Singapore or India have a more productive e-service sector in the Indo-Pacific.
answer_sources: World Bank: Vietnam Overview
subject: Economy

question_text_combined: In India, which one of the following is responsible for maintaining price stability by controlling inflation?
answer_explanation: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) is mandated with the primary objective of maintaining price stability while keeping in mind the objective of growth. It uses monetary policy tools (like repo rate adjustments) to control inflation.
answer_sources: RBI Act, 1934 (as amended by Finance Act 2016 for Monetary Policy Framework)
subject: Economy

question_text_combined: With reference to Non-Fungible Tokens (NFTs), consider the following statements: 1. They enable the digital representation of physical assets. 2. They are unique cryptographic tokens that exist on a blockchain. 3. They can be traded or exchanged at equivalency and therefore can be used as a medium of commercial transactions. Which of the statements given above are correct?
answer_explanation: Statement 1 is correct; NFTs can act as digital certificates of ownership for both digital and physical assets. Statement 2 is correct; they are unique, distinct tokens stored on a blockchain. Statement 3 is incorrect; the term 'non-fungible' explicitly means they cannot be exchanged at equivalency (unlike currency or Bitcoin), so they are not used as a standard medium of commercial exchange.
answer_sources: Ethereum Foundation documentation on NFTs
subject: Science & Tech

question_text_combined: Consider the following pairs: Reservoirs - States 1. Ghataprabha - Telangana 2. Gandhi Sagar - Madhya Pradesh 3. Indira Sagar - Andhra Pradesh 4. Maithon - Chhattisgarh. How many pairs given above are not correctly matched?
answer_explanation: Only Pair 2 is correct (Gandhi Sagar is on the Chambal River in MP). Pair 1 is incorrect; Ghataprabha is in Karnataka. Pair 3 is incorrect; Indira Sagar is in Madhya Pradesh. Pair 4 is incorrect; Maithon is in Jharkhand. Therefore, three pairs are not correctly matched.
answer_sources: Central Water Commission (CWC) National Register of Large Dams
subject: Geography

question_text_combined: In India, which one of the following compiles information on industrial disputes, closures, retrenchments and lay-offs in factories employing workers?
options_combined: Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade
answer_explanation: The Labour Bureau, under the Ministry of Labour & Employment, is responsible for collecting, compiling, and publishing labor statistics, including data on industrial disputes, closures, retrenchments, and lay-offs in India.
answer_sources: Ministry of Labour & Employment: Labour Bureau Mandate
subject: Economy

question_text_combined: In India, what is the role of the Coal Controller's Organization (CCO)? 1. CCO is the major source of Coal Statistics in Government of India. 2. It monitors progress of development of Captive Coal/Lignite blocks. 3. It hears any objection to the Government's notification relating to acquisition of coal-bearing areas. 4. It ensures that coal mining companies deliver the coal to end users in the prescribed time. Select the correct answer using the code given below:
answer_explanation: Statements 1, 2, and 3 are officially recognized functions of the Coal Controller's Organization (CCO) under the Ministry of Coal. Statement 4 is incorrect; ensuring timely commercial delivery of coal to end-users is a contractual and operational issue handled by coal companies and logistics entities (like Railways), not a regulatory function of the CCO.
answer_sources: Ministry of Coal: Functions of Coal Controller's Organisation
subject: Economy

question_text_combined: If a particular area is brought under the Fifth Schedule of the Constitution of India, which one of the following statements best reflects the consequence of it?
options_combined: This would prevent the transfer of land of tribal people to non-tribal people.
This would create a local self-governing body in that area.
This would convert that area into a Union Territory.
The State having such areas would be declared a Special Category State.
answer_explanation: The Fifth Schedule of the Constitution deals with the administration of Scheduled Areas. Its primary protective feature empowers the Governor to make regulations prohibiting or restricting the transfer of land by or among members of the Scheduled Tribes to non-tribals.
answer_sources: Constitution of India (Fifth Schedule, Para 5)
Samatha v. State of Andhra Pradesh (Supreme Court Judgment)
subject: Polity

question_text_combined: Consider the following statements : 1. The India Sanitation Coalition is a platform to promote sustainable sanitation and is funded by the Government of India and the World Health Organization. 2. The National Institute of Urban Affairs is an apex body of the Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs in Government of India and provides innovative solutions to address the challenges of Urban India. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
answer_explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect; the India Sanitation Coalition is a multi-stakeholder platform hosted by FICCI, not funded jointly by the GoI and WHO. Statement 2 is correct; the National Institute of Urban Affairs (NIUA) is an apex institute for research and capacity building under the Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs.
answer_sources: National Institute of Urban Affairs (NIUA) Official Mandate
FICCI India Sanitation Coalition
subject: Polity

question_text_combined: Which one of the following has been constituted under the Environment (Protection) Act, 1986?
answer_explanation: The Central Ground Water Authority (CGWA) was constituted under Section 3(3) of the Environment (Protection) Act, 1986, for the purpose of regulation and control of groundwater development and management. The Central Water Commission and Central Ground Water Board are attached/subordinate offices, not statutory bodies under this Act.
answer_sources: Ministry of Jal Shakti: CGWA mandate
Environment (Protection) Act, 1986 Section 3
subject: Environment

question_text_combined: With reference to the "United Nations Credentials Committee", consider the following statements: 1. It is a committee set up by the UN Security Council and works under its supervision. 2. It traditionally meets in March, June and September every year. 3. It assesses the credentials of all UN members before submitting a report to the General Assembly for approval. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
answer_explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect; the Credentials Committee is appointed by the UN General Assembly, not the Security Council. Statement 2 is incorrect; it typically meets at the beginning of each regular session of the UNGA, generally in September or November, not thrice a year. Statement 3 is correct; its mandate is to report to the Assembly on the credentials of representatives.
answer_sources: UN General Assembly Rules of Procedure (Rule 28)
subject: International Relations

question_text_combined: Which one of the following statements best describes the 'Polar Code'?
options_combined: It is the international code of safety for ships operating in polar waters.
It is the agreement of the countries around the North Pole regarding the demarcation of their territories in the polar region.
It is a set of norms to be followed by the countries whose scientists undertake research studies in the North Pole and South Pole.
It is a trade and security agreement of the member countries of the Arctic Council.
answer_explanation: The International Code for Ships Operating in Polar Waters (Polar Code) is a binding international framework adopted by the International Maritime Organization (IMO). It covers matters regarding ship design, construction, equipment, and training to ensure safety and environmental protection in the harsh polar environments.
answer_sources: International Maritime Organization (IMO) Documentation on Polar Code
subject: Environment

question_text_combined: With reference to the United Nations General Assembly, consider the following statements: 1. The UN General Assembly can grant observer status to the non-member States. 2. Inter-governmental organisations can seek observer status in the UN General Assembly. 3. Permanent Observers in the UN General Assembly can maintain missions at the UN headquarters. Which of the statements given above are correct?
answer_explanation: All statements are correct. The UNGA has the authority to grant observer status to non-member states (e.g., Holy See, State of Palestine) as well as inter-governmental organizations (e.g., European Union, African Union). These permanent observers have the right to maintain permanent missions at the UN Headquarters.
answer_sources: United Nations General Assembly - Observers Directory
subject: International Relations

question_text_combined: With reference to the "Tea Board" in India, consider the following statements: 1. The Tea Board is a statutory body. 2. It is a regulatory body attached to the Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare. 3. The Tea Board's Head Office is situated in Bengaluru. 4. The Board has overseas offices at Dubai and Moscow. Which of the statements given above are correct?
answer_explanation: Statement 1 is correct; the Tea Board is a statutory body created under the Tea Act, 1953. Statement 2 is incorrect; it functions under the Ministry of Commerce and Industry, not Agriculture. Statement 3 is incorrect; its headquarters is located in Kolkata, West Bengal. Statement 4 is correct; it maintains overseas offices in locations such as Dubai and Moscow to promote tea exports.
answer_sources: Tea Act, 1953
Ministry of Commerce and Industry: Tea Board of India Profile
subject: Economy

question_text_combined: Which one of the following best describes the term "greenwashing"?
options_combined: Conveying a false impression that a company's products are eco-friendly and environmentally sound
Non-inclusion of ecological/environmental costs in the Annual Financial Statements of a country
Ignoring the disastrous ecological consequences while undertaking infrastructure development
Making mandatory provisions for environmental costs in a government project/programme
answer_explanation: Greenwashing is a deceptive marketing or PR practice. It involves making unsubstantiated or misleading claims to deceive consumers into believing that a company's products, policies, or overall operations are environmentally friendly when they are not.
answer_sources: UNEP Reports on Corporate Sustainability
subject: Environment

question_text_combined: Consider the following statements: 1. High clouds primarily reflect solar radiation and cool the surface of the Earth. 2. Low clouds have a high absorption of infrared radiation emanating from the Earth's surface and thus cause warming effect. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
answer_explanation: Both statements are factually reversed. High clouds are typically thin and transparent; they let incoming solar radiation pass through but trap outgoing infrared radiation, leading to a net warming effect. Conversely, low clouds are thick and reflect incoming solar radiation back into space, causing a net cooling effect.
answer_sources: NCERT Class 11 Physical Geography: Atmospheric Circulation and Weather Systems
NASA Earth Observatory: Clouds and Radiation
subject: Geography

question_text_combined: Consider the following statements: 1. Bidibidi is a large refugee settlement in north-western Kenya. 2. Some people who fled from South Sudan civil war live in Bidibidi. 3. Some people who fled from civil war in Somalia live in Dadaab refugee complex in Kenya. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
answer_explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect; Bidibidi is a massive refugee settlement located in north-western Uganda, not Kenya. Statement 2 is correct; Bidibidi was established primarily to host refugees fleeing the brutal civil war in neighboring South Sudan. Statement 3 is correct; Dadaab is located in Kenya and historically hosts massive populations of Somali refugees.
answer_sources: UNHCR Reports on Uganda (Bidibidi) and Kenya (Dadaab)
subject: International Relations

question_text_combined: Consider the following countries: 1. Armenia 2. Azerbaijan 3. Croatia 4. Romania 5. Uzbekistan. Which of the above are members of the Organization of Turkic States?
answer_explanation: The Organization of Turkic States is an intergovernmental organization comprising countries with Turkic heritage. Its members are Azerbaijan, Kazakhstan, Kyrgyzstan, Turkey, and Uzbekistan. Armenia, Croatia, and Romania do not belong to this ethno-linguistic group and are not members.
answer_sources: Organization of Turkic States Official Member Directory
subject: International Relations

question_text_combined: Consider the following statements: 1. Gujarat has the largest solar park in India. 2. Kerala has a fully solar powered International Airport. 3. Goa has the largest floating solar photovoltaic project in India. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
answer_explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect; the Bhadla Solar Park, which is the largest in India (and the world), is located in Rajasthan, not Gujarat. Statement 2 is correct; Cochin International Airport (CIAL) in Kerala is the world's first fully solar-powered airport. Statement 3 is incorrect; the largest floating solar project commissioned in India around that time was in Ramagundam, Telangana, not Goa.
answer_sources: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy (MNRE) Project Reports
UNEP Champions of the Earth Award citation for CIAL
subject: Geography

question_text_combined: With reference to the United Nations Convention on the Law of the Sea, consider the following statements: 1. A coastal state has the right to establish the breadth of its territorial sea up to a limit not exceeding 12 nautical miles, measured from baselines. 2. Ships of all states, whether coastal or land-locked, enjoy the right of innocent passage through the territorial sea. 3. The Exclusive Economic Zone shall not extend beyond 200 nautical miles from the baselines. Which of the statements given above are correct?
answer_explanation: All three statements directly reflect standard provisions under UNCLOS. Article 3 guarantees the 12 nautical mile territorial sea limit. Article 17 guarantees the right of innocent passage for all states. Article 57 caps the Exclusive Economic Zone (EEZ) at 200 nautical miles from the baseline.
answer_sources: United Nations Convention on the Law of the Sea (UNCLOS) text
subject: International Relations

question_text_combined: Which one of the following pairs of countries fought over the Senkaku Islands dispute?
answer_explanation: The Senkaku Islands (known as Diaoyu Islands in China) are a group of uninhabited islands located in the East China Sea. They are administered by Japan but intensely claimed by the People's Republic of China, making them a major geopolitical flashpoint between the two nations.
answer_sources: Ministry of External Affairs (India) Background Briefs on East Asia
subject: International Relations

question_text_combined: Consider the following pairs: Country - Important reason for being in the news recently 1. Chad - Setting up of permanent military base by China 2. Guinea - Suspension of Constitution and Government by military 3. Lebanon - Severe and prolonged economic depression 4. Tunisia - Suspension of Parliament by President. How many pairs given above are correctly matched?
answer_explanation: Pair 1 is incorrect; Chad was in the news primarily due to the battlefield death of its long-serving president and the subsequent military takeover, not a Chinese base. Pair 2 is correct; Guinea suffered a military coup in 2021. Pair 3 is correct; Lebanon has been enduring one of the most severe global economic collapses since 2019. Pair 4 is correct; Tunisia's President controversially suspended parliament in 2021.
answer_sources: BBC/Reuters International News Archives (2021-2022)
subject: International Relations

question_text_combined: Consider the following pairs: Region often mentioned in the news - Country 1. Anatolia - Turkey 2. Amhara - Ethiopia 3. Cabo Delgado - Spain 4. Catalonia - Italy. How many pairs given above are correctly matched?
answer_explanation: Pair 1 is correct; Anatolia forms the Asian portion of Turkey. Pair 2 is correct; Amhara is a major regional state in Ethiopia, heavily involved in the Tigray conflict. Pair 3 is incorrect; Cabo Delgado is a province in Mozambique, suffering from an Islamist insurgency. Pair 4 is incorrect; Catalonia is an autonomous community within Spain, not Italy.
answer_sources: Oxford Atlas of the World
subject: Geography

question_text_combined: Certain species of which one of the following organisms are well known as cultivators of fungi?
answer_explanation: Leafcutter ants are famous in the animal kingdom for their highly evolved agricultural practices. They cut and forage leaves not to eat them directly, but to use them as a nutritional substrate to cultivate specialized fungi in their nests, which serves as their primary food source.
answer_sources: NCERT Class 11 Biology: Ecology
Nature Journal: Mutualism in fungus-growing ants
subject: Environment

question_text_combined: Consider the following pairs: Site of Ashoka's major rock edicts - Location in State of India 1. Dhauli - Odisha 2. Erragudi - Andhra Pradesh 3. Jaugada - Madhya Pradesh 4. Kalsi - Karnataka. How many pairs given above are correctly matched?
answer_explanation: Pair 1 is correct; Dhauli is located in Odisha. Pair 2 is correct; Erragudi is in Andhra Pradesh. Pair 3 is incorrect; Jaugada is located in Odisha, not Madhya Pradesh. Pair 4 is incorrect; Kalsi is located in Uttarakhand, not Karnataka. Thus, only two pairs are correct.
answer_sources: Upinder Singh: A History of Ancient and Early Medieval India
subject: History

question_text_combined: Consider the following pairs: King - Dynasty 1. Nannuka - Chandela 2. Jayashakti - Paramara 3. Nagabhata II - Gurjara-Pratihara 4. Bhoja - Rashtrakuta. How many pairs given above are correctly matched?
answer_explanation: Pair 1 is correct; Nannuka was the founder of the Chandela dynasty of Jejakabhukti. Pair 3 is correct; Nagabhata II was a significant ruler of the Gurjara-Pratihara dynasty. Pair 2 is incorrect; Jayashakti was a Chandela ruler, not a Paramara. Pair 4 is incorrect; Bhoja (Mihira Bhoja) was the greatest ruler of the Gurjara-Pratiharas, not a Rashtrakuta.
answer_sources: Satish Chandra: Medieval India
subject: History

question_text_combined: Which one of the following statements best describes the teaching of Ramanuja?
options_combined: The best means of salvation was devotion.
Vedas are eternal, self-existent and wholly authoritative.
Logical arguments were essential means for the highest bliss.
Salvation was to be obtained through meditation.
answer_explanation: Ramanujacharya, an 11th-century theologian and chief proponent of Vishishtadvaita Vedanta, strongly taught that intense devotion (Bhakti) to Lord Vishnu, coupled with total surrender (prapatti), was the ultimate and most accessible path to salvation (Moksha).
answer_sources: NCERT Class 12 History: Bhakti-Sufi Traditions
subject: History

question_text_combined: Consider the following statements: 1. Somnath Temple is one of the Jyotirlinga shrines. 2. A description of Somnath Temple was given by Al-Biruni. 3. Pran Pratishtha of Somnath Temple (installation of the present day temple) was done by President S. Radhakrishnan. Which of the statements given above are correct?
answer_explanation: Statement 1 is correct; Somnath is revered as the first of the 12 Jyotirlinga shrines of Shiva. Statement 2 is correct; the 11th-century Arab scholar Al-Biruni left a detailed description of the temple's wealth, which attracted Mahmud of Ghazni's invasion. Statement 3 is incorrect; the Pran Pratishtha of the newly built temple in 1951 was performed by Dr. Rajendra Prasad, India's first President, not S. Radhakrishnan.
answer_sources: A.L. Basham: The Wonder That Was India
PIB Archives
subject: History

question_text_combined: Which one of the following statements best describes the role of B cells and T cells in the human body?
options_combined: They protect the body from environmental allergens.
They alleviate the body's pain and inflammation.
They act as immunosuppressants in the body.
They protect the body from the diseases caused by pathogens.
answer_explanation: B cells and T cells are highly specialized lymphocytes (white blood cells) that form the core of the adaptive immune system. B cells produce targeted antibodies, and T cells directly destroy infected cells, acting collectively to identify and eliminate invading pathogens like viruses and bacteria.
answer_sources: NCERT Class 12 Biology: Human Health and Disease
subject: Science & Tech

question_text_combined: Consider the following statements: 1. Other than those made by humans, nanoparticles do not exist in nature. 2. Nanoparticles of some metallic oxides are used in the manufacture of some cosmetics. 3. Nanoparticles of some commercial products which enter the environment are unsafe for humans. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
answer_explanation: Statement 1 is completely false; nanoparticles are abundant in nature, occurring in volcanic ash, sea spray, and smoke. Statement 2 is correct; titanium dioxide and zinc oxide nanoparticles are widely used in sunscreens and cosmetics. Statement 3 is correct; engineered nanoparticles (like nano-silver) released into aquatic ecosystems can bioaccumulate and present severe toxicity risks.
answer_sources: WHO/UNEP reports on Nanotoxicology
subject: Science & Tech

question_text_combined: Consider the following statements: DNA Barcoding can be a tool to: 1. assess the age of a plant or animal. 2. distinguish among species that look alike. 3. identify undesirable animal or plant materials in processed foods. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
answer_explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect; DNA barcoding relies on examining short, standardized gene sequences to identify a species, not an individual's chronological age. Statement 2 is correct; its primary use is taxonomy and distinguishing cryptic species that appear morphologically identical. Statement 3 is correct; it is highly effective in food forensics to detect adulteration or mislabeling of organic matter.
answer_sources: NCBI: Applications of DNA Barcoding
subject: Science & Tech

question_text_combined: Consider the following: 1. Carbon monoxide 2. Nitrogen oxide 3. Ozone 4. Sulphur dioxide. Excess of which of the above in the environment is/are cause(s) of acid rain?
answer_explanation: Acid rain results primarily from the emission of sulphur dioxide (SO2) and nitrogen oxides (NOx) into the atmosphere. These gases react with water, oxygen, and other atmospheric chemicals to form highly corrosive sulfuric and nitric acids. Carbon monoxide and ozone do not form the acid base of precipitation.
answer_sources: NCERT Class 11 Chemistry: Environmental Chemistry
subject: Environment

question_text_combined: With reference to the Indian laws about wildlife protection, consider the following statements: 1. Wild animals are the sole property of the government. 2. When a wild animal is declared protected, such animal is entitled for equal protection whether it is found in protected areas or outside. 3. Apprehension of a protected wild animal becoming a danger to human life is sufficient ground for its capture or killing. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
answer_explanation: Statement 1 is correct; under Section 39 of the Wildlife Protection Act (WPA) 1972, every wild animal is declared to be the property of the State/Central Government. Statement 2 is correct; a Schedule I animal enjoys identical legal protection regardless of its physical location. Statement 3 is incorrect; mere 'apprehension' is not sufficient. The Chief Wildlife Warden must be satisfied that the animal cannot be tranquilized or translocated before ordering it killed.
answer_sources: Wildlife Protection Act, 1972 (Section 11, Section 39)
Supreme Court Judgments on Wildlife Protection
subject: Environment

question_text_combined: Consider the following statements: 1. St. Francis Xavier was one of the founding members of the Jesuit Order. 2. St. Francis Xavier died in Goa and a church is dedicated to him there. 3. The Feast of St. Francis Xavier is celebrated in Goa each year. Which of the statements given above are correct?
answer_explanation: Statement 1 is correct; St. Francis Xavier co-founded the Society of Jesus (Jesuit Order) along with Ignatius of Loyola. Statement 2 is incorrect; he died of a fever on Shangchuan Island, China. His body was preserved and later brought to Goa. Statement 3 is correct; his feast is celebrated annually on December 3rd in Goa.
answer_sources: Historical Archives of Goa
subject: History

question_text_combined: Yogavasistha was translated into Persian by Nizamuddin Panipati during the reign of:
answer_explanation: The Yogavasistha, an important syncretic philosophical text, was translated into Persian by Nizamuddin Panipati. This was executed under the patronage of the Mughal Emperor Akbar, who established a dedicated translation bureau (Maktab Khana) to translate significant Sanskrit works into Persian.
answer_sources: Satish Chandra: Medieval India
Mughal Court Translation Records
subject: History
